input.prompt:
I need to understand the structure of existing AI provider packages in the Vercel AI SDK monorepo so I can create a new provider. Please explore thoroughly:

1. Look at the directory structure of a simple provider package (e.g., packages/openai or packages/anthropic) - what files are in the root and src directories
2. Look at the package.json of an existing provider to understand the exports, dependencies, scripts, etc.
3. Look at a provider that supports image generation - find how image models are implemented. Check packages/openai or others for image model implementations.
4. Look at how the provider factory function works (e.g., createOpenAI or similar)
5. Look at the @ai-sdk/provider package to understand the ImageModelV2 interface (or whatever interface image models implement)
6. Look at the tsconfig.json patterns used in provider packages
7. Look at the root tsconfig.json to see how package references are listed

Report back with detailed findings including file paths and key code snippets.

content.text:
I need to understand the structure of existing AI provider packages in the Vercel AI SDK monorepo so I can create a new provider. Please explore thoroughly:

1. Look at the directory structure of a simple provider package (e.g., packages/openai or packages/anthropic) - what files are in the root and src directories
2. Look at the package.json of an existing provider to understand the exports, dependencies, scripts, etc.
3. Look at a provider that supports image generation - find how image models are implemented. Check packages/openai or others for image model implementations.
4. Look at how the provider factory function works (e.g., createOpenAI or similar)
5. Look at the @ai-sdk/provider package to understand the ImageModelV2 interface (or whatever interface image models implement)
6. Look at the tsconfig.json patterns used in provider packages
7. Look at the root tsconfig.json to see how package references are listed

Report back with detailed findings including file paths and key code snippets.

## New `@ai-sdk/pixelart` Provider Package

### Files Created (15 new files, 1 modified):

**Package infrastructure** (root-level configs):
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `packages/pixelart/package.json` | Package metadata, exports, dependencies (`@ai-sdk/provider`, `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`) |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.json` | TypeScript config extending `@vercel/ai-tsconfig`, references provider packages |
| `packages/pixelart/tsconfig.build.json` | Build-specific TS config (disables composite) |
| `packages/pixelart/tsup.config.ts` | Build config producing CJS + ESM with DTS |
| `packages/pixelart/turbo.json` | Turborepo task config |
| `packages/pixelart/vitest.node.config.js` | Node test environment |
| `packages/pixelart/vitest.edge.config.js` | Edge runtime test environment |

**Source files** (`src/`):
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/index.ts` | Public exports: `createPixelArt`, `pixelart`, types |
| `src/pixelart-provider.ts` | `createPixelArt()` factory function returning `PixelArtProvider` (implements `ProviderV3`). Uses `https://api.pixelart.ai/v1` base URL, `PIXELART_API_KEY` env var. Throws `NoSuchModelError` for language/embedding models. |
| `src/pixelart-image-model.ts` | `PixelArtImageModel` class implementing `ImageModelV3`. Supports `size` ("256x256"/"512x512"/"1024x1024"), `style` ("pixel"/"retro"/"8bit"), and `pixelDensity` (1-4) via provider options. Posts to `/images/generations`. |
| `src/pixelart-image-settings.ts` | `PixelArtImageModelId` type with `"pixelart-standard"`, `"pixelart-hd"`, and extensible `string` |
| `src/pixelart-error.ts` | Error response handler with JSON schema |
| `src/version.ts` | Build-time injected version constant |

**Tests**:
| File | Tests |
|---|---|
| `src/pixelart-image-model.test.ts` | 5 tests: spec version, model ID, provider name, maxImagesPerCall, custom model IDs |
| `src/pixelart-provider.test.ts` | 5 tests: image model creation, imageModel(), NoSuchModelError for language/embedding, spec version |

**Modified**:
- `tsconfig.json` (root) — added `{ "path": "packages/pixelart" }` reference

### All 10 tests pass ✅
